bullets.text: Q3 CR 92.4, improving 8 pts from Q2; YTD CR fell to 97.2 from 99.7 at Q2 end; Personal Lines Q3 CR 91.2 (vs. 99.5 in Q2); profitability target of 96.0 within reach heading into Q4.

Companywide NPW up 20% YTD; Personal Lines Q3 NPW $12.7B (+23% YOY); intentional new business slowdown: Personal Lines new auto apps down ~20% YOY in both Agency and Direct channels to prioritize profitability.

Personal auto rate revisions in 25 states during Q3 (+4 pts); YTD personal auto rate increases ~16 pts; commercial auto (non-TNC) rates up ~12% YTD with additional rate planned; roughly 6 pts of 2022 rate and 4 pts of 2021 rate still earning in.
Personal Lines PIFs 25.5 million, up 11% YOY; PLE improved ~8% YOY as retention gains offset intentional new business pullback; commercial Lines PIFs up 7% YOY despite freight market pressure.

Personal auto severity +5% YOY in Q3, sharply moderating from +12% in Q2; Florida HB 837 reserve impact adequately recognized; commercial auto adverse prior-year development added 3.8 pts; vehicle repair cost trends showing some stabilization.

Debt-to-capital maintained below 30%; no debt maturities until 2027; Q3 investment return -0.2% (fixed income -0.1%, equity -3.1%); conservative posture maintained with overweight Treasuries and underweight credit/equities.

BOP launched in 2 new states (43 total); BOP online buy capability launched through BusinessQuote Explorer; heavy truck roadside assistance in 46 states covering ~92% of eligible vehicles; commercial product expansion continuing.

Commercial Lines CR 99.1 (9.4 pts worse YOY) from rising claims costs and adverse development; for-hire transportation premiums declining with freight market contraction; business auto and contractor BMTs growing and offsetting.

Property Q3 CR 88.2 (down ~37 pts YOY as no Ian equivalent); cat losses 18 pts (vs. 35 pts in Q3 2022); $23M reinsurance recoveries (3.6 CR pts); YTD Property CR 108.9, improving 8.2 pts from 2022 YTD.
Property PIFs 3.0M (+7% YOY); growth states up 18% while high-volatility states down ~6%; 115,000 Florida property non-renewals initiated to rebalance portfolio; YTD property rate increases ~9 pts.
